message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first poll sleeps in mux + watcher shutdown paths (tick 283)

4 ambient `runtime_compat::sleep` call sites in `main.rs` poll loops
migrated to `sleep_with_cx` with cancel-handling:

**`stop_mux_server_processes` poll (L9204 area):**

- `sleep(Duration::from_millis(200))` → `sleep_with_cx(&mux_stop_cx, ...)`
- cx-cancel during wait returns `Err("Cx cancelled while waiting for
  mux server shutdown")` instead of running out the full stop_timeout

**`wait_for_mux_ready` poll (L9239 area):**

- Reuses the existing tick-230 `cx` binding from the list_panes_with_cx
  site (no new binding)
- cx-cancel returns `Err("Cx cancelled while waiting for mux server
  readiness")` — integrates with mux-ready timeout error handling

**`ft watcher stop` graceful poll + SIGKILL wait (L26636 + L26656):**

- Both use a shared `watcher_stop_cx` binding declared above the
  while-loop
- Graceful poll: cx-cancel breaks the loop (treating as cancel-
  equivalent, flowing into the `--force` SIGKILL branch if set)
- SIGKILL settle wait: `let _ = sleep_with_cx(...).await` swallows the
  Err — the SIGKILL has already been sent, so the wait is best-effort

All sites use `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`. These are
the last remaining poll-loop sleeps in main.rs production paths that
should observe cx-cancel:

  - `distributed_agent_sleep_with_shutdown` (tick 282)
  - `stop_mux_server_processes` poll (this tick)
  - `wait_for_mux_ready` poll (this tick)
  - `ft watcher stop` graceful + SIGKILL (this tick)

Remaining `runtime_compat::sleep` sites in main.rs are either:
- Inside `select!` biased arms (polled by select, no explicit await)
- In `task::spawn(async move { ... })` bodies (deferred migration)
- In sim/replay render-delay code where cx-cancel adds no value

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 97 (187-283).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 178 sites (174 before + 4).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
